title: Best Practices - Organizational Cyber Readiness to the Post-Quantum Age
summary: Israel National Cyber Directorate best-practices document setting out the threat from quantum-computing-based attacks and recommended courses of action for organizations to achieve cyber readiness for the post-quantum age, including migration to quantum-resistant cryptography.

title: Banking system preparedness for the quantum computing era (Banking Supervision letter)
summary: Bank of Israel Banking Supervision Department supervisory letter (7 January 2025) directing banking corporations to prepare for the quantum computing era, including assessing infrastructure suitability for post-quantum encryption, mapping cryptographic inventory, and submitting an initial preparedness plan within a year.

title: Preparedness for Dealing with Encryption in the Quantum Computing Era (National Digital Agency guidelines)
summary: Israel National Digital Agency policy guidance directing government ministries and public bodies to prepare for the post-quantum era, explicitly naming Post Quantum Cryptography (PQC), warning that quantum computers will break encryption such as RSA, and requiring infrastructure mapping, data-exposure assessment, and a move toward quantum-resistant encryption.

title: Intelligence Report – Quantum Computers and Their Impact on Information Security
issuingOrg: Ministry of Communications (Israel), Sectoral Cyber Unit
summary: Israel Ministry of Communications (Engineering Administration, Sectoral Cyber Unit) intelligence report dated 1 July 2025 analysing how quantum computers can break common encryption algorithms and the resulting risk to information security, framing the need for quantum-resistant cryptographic measures.

title: Israel National Cyber Security Strategy 2025
summary: Israel National Cyber Directorate national cyber security strategy (February 2025) setting Israel's cyber-resilience vision and objectives; multiple official sources state it places 'readiness for the quantum era' as a strategic axis, but an explicit post-quantum-cryptography reference inside the document text could not be verified during this pass, so it is flagged for human review.
